channel.title: Globehint
channel.description: Deep, honest travel guides for every destination on earth.

item.title: Bremen Travel Guide
item.description: Forget Berlin's grit or Munich's polish. Bremen pairs medieval fairy-tale quarters with a rowdy, maritime grit and an obsession with local beer.

item.title: Hamburg Travel Guide
item.description: Germany's fierce, water-obsessed northern hub where grit meets high culture along legendary canals and harbor docks.

item.title: Lüneburg Travel Guide
item.description: A gorgeous medieval salt-mining town of brick Gothic facades, crooked half-timbered lanes, and water-side beer gardens that escaped WWII entirely unscathed.

item.title: Bad Ischl Travel Guide
item.description: Trade the Alpine crowds of Hallstatt for the grand imperial spa town of Bad Ischl, famous for its thermal waters, royal palace, and world-class pastries.

item.title: Gosausee Alpine Lakes Travel Guide
item.description: A dramatic trio of glacial lakes framed by the jagged peaks of the Dachstein Alps, offering the perfect outdoor escape just outside Hallstatt.

item.title: Dachstein Krippenstein Travel Guide
item.description: Escape the crowded lakeside streets of Hallstatt for the jaw-dropping dramatic heights of the Dachstein Alps, complete with subterranean ice worlds and a thrilling walk over a 400-meter drop.

item.title: Berlin Travel Guide
item.description: A gritty, unvarnished look at Germany's capital from Cold War scars to world-class techno and cheap street eats.

item.title: Munich Travel Guide
item.description: Bavaria's grand capital pairs stately neoclassical architecture and world-class museums with the unhurried, beer-garden-drenched ease of Gemütlichkeit.

item.title: Sachsenhausen Memorial Travel Guide
item.description: A haunting, deeply educational encounter with World War II and Cold War history at one of the earliest Nazi concentration camps.

item.title: Potsdam Travel Guide
item.description: A grand playground of Prussian kings, spectacular royal estates, and vast formal gardens located just a short train ride from the capital.

item.title: Salzburg Travel Guide
item.description: A Baroque masterpiece framed by alpine peaks, Salzburg effortlessly blends classical musical heritage with a vibrant, historic beer culture.

item.title: Dachau Memorial Site Travel Guide
item.description: A deeply moving, heavy, yet essential look at Germany's first concentration camp, located just outside Munich.

item.title: Hallstatt Travel Guide
item.description: Discover the fairytale alpine village of Hallstatt. Learn how to beat the daytime crowds, find the best lake views, and explore the world's oldest salt mine.

item.title: Neuschwanstein Travel Guide
item.description: Escape the city to see King Ludwig II's fairytale masterpiece nestled in the Bavarian Alps.

item.title: Berchtesgaden & Eagle's Nest Travel Guide
item.description: Scale the Bavarian Alps to explore Adolf Hitler's former mountain retreat, stand among jagged alpine peaks, and uncover the dark history of the Obersalzberg complex.

item.title: Untersberg Mountain Travel Guide
item.description: Escape the city for dramatic alpine views, high-altitude hiking trails, and a legendary mountain peak straddling the Austrian-German border.
